title: Oddie is a peer subscriber on AMS, not an orchestrator
content: Oddie joins TinCan conversations as a polymorphic subscriber per AMS's subscriber model. He is not an orchestrator, coordinator, or privileged participant. The wire does not know or care that Oddie is a guide. This is consistent with ams://canon/constraints/permanent-non-goals.

title: Oddie lives at TinCan layer, not AMS wire
content: Per ams://canon/decisions/D0026-two-worker-topology-ams-substrate-tincan-ui-layer, Oddie's interpretation logic lives in TinCan (UI layer), not in AMS (substrate). The substrate does not know Oddie exists. Correct altitude.

title: Real-time stream interpretation is the flagship use case
content: Designing for the hardest surface (highest density, tightest brevity, fastest detection) sharpens every other surface. Other use cases (audit, mentorship, strategic translation) inherit the constraints; the flagship sets them.

title: Oddie speaks two languages on two surfaces
content: Agent-facing: structured observations as tokens on his own stream (AMS wire protocol). Human-facing: narrative clarity in the TinCan portal sidebar (voice canon governs). Same character, different register.

title: Interpreter-subscriber is a new AMS subscriber pattern
content: Oddie is the first subscriber whose purpose is to interpret other subscribers' streams for a human audience. Not an agent performing work, not a logger — an interpreter translating machine-speed to human-speed. The pattern is replicable with different guides and different knowledge bases.

title: Throughput at high stream rates is unknown
content: How Oddie performs with many concurrent high-token-rate streams is untested. At what point does interpretation latency exceed the value of real-time annotation? Requires load testing.

title: Token budget for Oddie's own operation is unknown
content: Oddie's interpretation consumes tokens. At high stream rates, cost may be significant. Budget and scaling model require real workload data.

title: Portal UX for Oddie's output is undesigned
content: Sidebar chat, stream annotation, query interface are conceptual. Layout, interaction patterns, annotation density, dismissibility require design and user testing.

title: Multi-conversation context isolation is undecided
content: Can Oddie participate in multiple conversations simultaneously? Per-conversation state vs stateless observation is an open architectural question.

title: Detection-only inherited from upstream
content: Oddie's detection-only constraint per klappy://canon/constraints/critic-cannot-be-resolver is inherited by reference, not restated. The TinCan integration spec does not weaken or reinterpret it.

title: Voice canon inherited from upstream
content: Oddie's voice register per klappy://canon/voice/oddie-the-river-guide governs all human-facing output. The TinCan integration does not define its own voice — it inherits.

title: Integration spec at ams://docs/oddie/tincan-real-time-guide
content: Architectural placement, dual-surface role, portal interaction surface, capability sources, detection-only reference, open questions. Confidence: proposal.

title: Flagship observation at ams://canon/observations/oddie-flagship-use-case
content: Why real-time stream interpretation is the flagship: maximally tests every voice constraint. Establishes interpreter-subscriber pattern.

title: Upstream klappy.dev PR #179 carries voice and methodology canon
content: All voice specification, methodology-personification, critic-cannot-be-resolver, and supporting documents live upstream at https://github.com/klappy/klappy.dev/pull/179. This downstream PR references them by URI.

title: Fresh-context review agent required before merge
content: Both PRs require validation by a separate fresh-context review agent per klappy://canon/principles/verification-requires-fresh-context. Writing agent cannot validate its own output.
